entry.title: Rick Garotti Scarf Presentation
entry.content:
On the 25th of November 2020, Bundoora Scouts was pleased to host the new Banyule Council Mayor, Councillor Rick Garotti to be presented with the State Scouts Scarf. We are extremely grateful for the support that Banyule Council, and our local councillor and now Mayor, Cr Rick Garotti, has given to Bundoora Scout Group over the past few years. We look forward to many more opportunities to help Banyule Council build resilience and leadership in the local community.

01 Rope Bridge Building 02 Rope Bridge Building 03 Rope Bridge Building 04 Rope Bridge Building 09 Rope Bridge Building 10 Rope Bridge Building 11 Chris Talking Ricks Ear Off 12 Rope Bridge Building 14 Not Rope Bridge Building 15 Not Rope Bridge Building 16 Rope Bridge Building 18 Rope Bridge Building 19 Scarf Presentation 20 Scarf Presentation 21 Scarf Presentation 22 Scarf Presentation 23 Scarf Presentation 24 Scarf Presentation 25 Scarf Presentation 26 Scarf Presentation

A few words from Cr Rick Garotti.

One of our Cub Scouts Daniel later discussed future Bundoora Scout Group projects with Cr Rick Garotti.
entry.summary: On the 25th of November 2020, Bundoora Scouts was pleased to host the new Banyule Council Mayor, Councillor Rick Garotti to be presented with the State Scouts Scarf. We are extremely grateful for the support that Banyule Council, and our local councillor and now Mayor, Cr Rick Garotti, has given to Bundoora Scout Group over the past few years. We look forward to many more opportunities to help Banyule Council build resilience and leadership in the local community.

entry.title: Taneesha’s Queen’s Scout
entry.content:
CONGRATULATIONS TO: Taneesha Pattison

Bundoora Scout Group is very excited to announce that, for the first time since 1992, we have a Queen’s Scout. We are very proud to announce that Taneesha Pattison has been approved for the prestigious award having completed all the technical requirements and undergoing a rigorous character check, Taneesha will, sometime in February be invested as a Queens Scout. An amazing feat during a year of Covid and (almost) on her own.

A massive congratulations to Taneesha for all her hard work from everyone at Bundoora Scouts.

taneesha

entry.title: Clean Up Australia Day
entry.content:
Bundoora Scouts joined local volunteer conservation group Friends of the Wildlife Reserves for Clean Up Australia Day 2020 at Gresswell Forest Nature Conservation Reserve. Much of the local storm drainage system ends up in Gresswell Forest and unfortunately a lot of rubbish too. All of the volunteers split in to three groups, with Bundoora Scouts tackling the North West corner wetlands leading to Salt Creek.

01 Getting ready to go 02 Heading to our patch 03 Cleaning 04 Cleaning 05 Cleaning 06 Gumboots required 07 Practicing camouflage 08 River crossing 09 Cleaning 10 Cleaning
entry.summary: Bundoora Scouts joined local volunteer conservation group Friends of the Wildlife Reserves for Clean Up Australia Day 2020 at Gresswell Forest Nature Conservation Reserve. Much of the local storm drainage system ends up in Gresswell Forest and unfortunately a lot of rubbish too. All of the volunteers split in to three groups, with Bundoora Scouts tackling the North West corner wetlands leading to Salt Creek.

entry.title: Joeys and Beavers Meet
entry.content:
Our Joey Scout mob held a sleep over at the hall as practise for Kangaree the following weekend. Before bed we cooked pancakes in the new kitchen and watched a movie. The next morning, we up early for breakfast followed by a very special video call with a Beaver Scout pack in England! As it was also Cleanup Australia day, we did our part to cleanup N.J. Telfer reserve which surrounds the Bundoora Scout Hall.

01 video call 02 video call 03 video call 04 cleanup australia

Thanks to Generation-e and Biagio LaRosa for providing the TV and also loaning us a Poly EagleEye IV USB Camera and Crestron UC Video Conference Smart Soundbar. Through the generous support of our community we’re able to offer amazing experiences to our youth members.
entry.summary: Our Joey Scout mob held a sleep over at the hall as practise for Kangaree the following weekend. Before bed we cooked pancakes in the new kitchen and watched a movie. The next morning, we up early for breakfast followed by a very special video call with a Beaver Scout pack in England! As it was also Cleanup Australia day, we did our part to cleanup N.J. Telfer reserve which surrounds the Bundoora Scout Hall.

entry.title: Cub Scouts End of Year
entry.content:
Bundoora Cub Scout Pack held our end of year meeting at Latitude in Bundoora. Much bouncing was had combined with dodgeball, basketball, ninja warrior and a high wire obstacle course. We also invested our newest member Adam and welcomed Syrus to the pack.

Everyone at Bundoora Scout Group wishes you a Merry Christmas, Happy New Year and safe travels over the school holidays.

See you all in 2020 for more adventures!

Latitude 001 Latitude 002 Latitude 010 Latitude 040 Latitude 041 Latitude 060 Latitude 069 Latitude 104 Latitude 109 Latitude 117 Latitude 125
entry.summary: Bundoora Cub Scout Pack held our end of year meeting at Latitude in Bundoora. Much bouncing was had combined with dodgeball, basketball, ninja warrior and a high wire obstacle course. We also invested our newest member Adam and welcomed Syrus to the pack.

entry.title: Group Camp
entry.content:
Bundoora Scout Group held our end of year group camp on the 6th, 7th and 8th of December at the Bundoora Scout Hall with all sections.

Friday evening saw everyone pitch in to set up tents and prepare a BBQ dinner. Followed by kids, big and little, bouncing around on the jumping castle with a movie before turning in.

Early Saturday morning we boarded a bus, train and tram to the Shrine of Remembrance on St Kilda Road in Melbourne. On arrival we were greeted by our guide Barry who gave the group a very personal tour of the building and surrounds. We were also thankful to be able to invest our newest Joey member Blake and award the Scouts Pioneer Cord (Red) to Will in the Crypt beneath the Sanctuary. On return to our hall, and after some more bouncing, we all enjoyed a roast dinner prepared by Mang (Sarah) in our amazing new kitchen.

Sunday morning everyone pitched in again to pack up tents after breakfast, before heading home (to catch up on sleep).

Many thanks to; our Shrine of Remembrance guide Barry, Eagle’s (Kirsty) awesome planning, and our leaders Akela (Hamish), Bagheera (Peter), Kangaroo (Melissa), Mang (Sarah), Platypus (Sue), and our group leader Rafiki (Chris).

001 Campsite 004 Too Early 005 Ready to go 016 Waiting for the bus 019 Waiting for the bus 020 The only person to get a good nights sleep 026 We are not hitchhiking 035 On the train 040 On the train 043 On the train 046 Super Excited to be at Flinder St 050 Shrine 054 Shrine 060 World War I Uniforms 061 World War I Equipment 064 World War I Uniform 070 Shrine 071 Pausing to Reflect 073 Paying our Respects 074 Sanctuary 076 Sanctuary 079 Sanctuary 080 Sanctuary 081 Shrine 082 Eternal Flame 083 Eternal Flame 085 Eternal Flame 086 Shrine 091 The Driver 093 The Driver 094 Wipers 096 Wipers 098 The Man with the Donkey 099 The Man with the Donkey 104 Lunch 105 Shrine 107 Medals 110 Crypt 114 Joeys Investment 119 Joeys Investment 120 Joeys Investment 123 Joeys Investment 125 Joeys Investment 126 Scout Pioneer Cord 127 Scout Pioneer Cord 128 Scout Pioneer Cord 131 Shrine 133 Our Guide Barry 135 Shrine 138 Police Memorial 141 Fountain 145 Australia 108 and Eureka 146 Floral Clock 150 Lunch 153 Lunch 161 Lunch 163 Lunch 164 Lunch 166 Arts Centre Spire 167 Yarra and MCG 168 Heading Home 170 XTrap 100 173 Train home 178 Train home
entry.summary: Bundoora Scout Group held our end of year group camp on the 6th, 7th and 8th of December at the Bundoora Scout Hall with all sections.

entry.title: Scout Hall Working Bee
entry.content:
Thank you parents and members of Bundoora Scout Group that helped on Saturday. Our hall and grounds are looking better than ever thanks to your efforts. Thanks again Chris Parr for coordinating our working bee day.

Everyone pitched in to help:

  • Finish the kitchen and move stuff back in
  • Label all the boxes in the resource room
  • Pack tents away
  • Mow the grass
  • Wash the windows
  • Finish emptying the Rover Den store
  • Clean up all debris around the new fence
  • Cut back bushes.

One of our Cubs, Daniel, recorded surprise man-on-the-street interviews of everyone hard at work cooking lunch, creating a lockable storage space for a weekly hall hire group, cleaning up, and organising equipment in our new kitchen. Thanks Daniel!
entry.summary: Thank you parents and members of Bundoora Scout Group that helped on Saturday. Our hall and grounds are looking better than ever thanks to your efforts. Thanks again Chris Parr for coordinating our working bee day.
